skill.name: claude-opus-4-5-migration
skill.description: Helps migrate Claude prompts, model identifiers, and API usage from Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, or Opus 4.1 to Opus 4.5.
skill.summary: Updates Claude model references and prompt guidance for Opus 4.5 migrations.
skill.author: DennisLiuCk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium alerts, but review found documentation-only Markdown rather than executable code. Backtick, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and entropy detections are false positives from inline code examples, ordinary English substrings, and bilingual content.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Not Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed files contain migration instructions, model identifiers, and fenced examples, with no executable Ruby, shell scripts, or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax appears inside Markdown text and fenced examples only. No file contains runnable script logic or command execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Detections Are Text Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak algorithm findings align with natural-language words and headings, not cryptographic functions. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation being used by this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found prose about migration and design quality, not cryptographic code. The line references contain words that can trigger simple substring matching.

low_findings.title: Entropy And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Documentation Signals
low_findings.description: High entropy warnings appear caused by mixed Chinese and English documentation. System reconnaissance alerts do not correspond to commands that collect host, user, process, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are readable Markdown with bilingual text and no encoded payload. The cited reconnaissance lines are ordinary prompt guidance, not host inspection commands.

content.value_statement: Claude model upgrades can leave stale model strings, beta headers, and prompts. This skill guides a focused migration to Opus 4.5 across code and prompt files.
actual_capabilities: Identifies Claude Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, and Opus 4.1 model identifiers for replacement.
Maps target Opus 4.5 model strings for Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry.
Guides removal of the unsupported 1M context beta header for Opus 4.5 migrations.
Provides effort parameter examples for Python SDK, TypeScript SDK, and raw API usage.
Suggests optional prompt adjustments for tool use, code exploration, frontend quality, and reasoning language.
limitations: It does not migrate Haiku model references.
It relies on the model identifiers documented in the skill files.
It provides guidance and snippets, not an automated migration script.
Optional prompt changes should be applied only when the user reports matching behavior issues.

use_cases.title: Update API Integrations
use_cases.description: Replace older Claude model identifiers and review beta headers before moving production calls to Opus 4.5.

use_cases.title: Refresh Prompt Libraries
use_cases.description: Adjust system prompts that over-trigger tools, over-engineer solutions, or use terms that affect Opus 4.5 behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan Platform Migration Reviews
use_cases.description: Compare Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure model string formats during a cross-platform upgrade.

prompt_templates.title: Find Model References
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this project for Claude Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, and Opus 4.1 model references. List each file and the replacement needed for Opus 4.5.

prompt_templates.title: Update One Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Migrate this Claude API integration to Opus 4.5. Update the model string, remove unsupported beta headers, and summarize the changes.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Prompt Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this system prompt for Opus 4.5 behavior issues. Suggest only changes related to tool overuse, over-engineering, code exploration, or reasoning terms.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Multi-Platform Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this migration plan for Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry. Check model identifiers, effort settings, unsupported headers, and prompt changes.

output_examples.input: A project still uses a Sonnet 4.5 model string in a TypeScript service.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the service file, recommends the Opus 4.5 replacement for the detected platform, and notes any beta header changes.

output_examples.input: A migrated agent calls tools too often after the model change.
output_examples.output: The response recommends softer tool-triggering language and explains which prompt lines should change.

output_examples.input: A team wants to compare provider-specific model names before release.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the correct Opus 4.5 identifiers for each supported provider and flags unsupported Haiku migration requests.

best_practices: Start by inventorying all model strings before editing files.
Apply optional prompt snippets only when a matching behavior issue appears.
Document every model, beta header, effort, and prompt change made during migration.
anti_patterns: Do not migrate Haiku model references with this skill.
Do not append prompt snippets without matching the existing prompt structure.
Do not keep unsupported beta headers when moving calls to Opus 4.5.

faq.question: What does this skill migrate?
faq.answer: It helps migrate prompts and code from Claude Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, or Opus 4.1 to Opus 4.5.

faq.question: Does it support Haiku migrations?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly says not to migrate Haiku models.

faq.question: Which platforms are covered?
faq.answer: It documents model strings for Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry.

faq.question: Does it change files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration guidance that an assistant can apply during a coding task.

faq.question: When should prompt snippets be added?
faq.answer: Add them only when the user asks for prompt tuning or reports the specific behavior issue described.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The scanned files are Markdown guidance files. Static alerts were false positives and no executable dangerous behavior was found.
